+  Problema DUPLO:
+  (a) Downloader carrega ZIP inteiro em memГіria вҶ’ OOM em Fly.io.

+  CorreГ§ГЈo obrigatГіria вҖ” Downloader:
+    - Streaming download para NamedTemporaryFile em /data/tmp/
+    - Chunked transfer (64KB chunks)
+    - MAX_DOWNLOAD_SIZE configurГЎvel (default 1GB), abort + FAILED

+    - Content-Length validation: comparar bytes escritos vs header

+      last_error captura o motivo)
+    - ZIP integrity: zipfile.testzip() apГіs download completo

+    - content_fingerprint (SHA-256): armazenado para audit trail e
+      detecГ§ГЈo de re-download diferente, NГғO para validaГ§ГЈo contra

+  Problema (refinado Round 2): O SourceRouter Round 1 conflata duas
+  preocupaГ§Гөes distintas:
+    (1) DISCOVERY: onde obter metadados/listagem de ediГ§Гөes
+    (2) DOWNLOAD: onde obter o ZIP/XML payload
+  
+  INLABS pode responder discovery (listar ediГ§Гөes) mas falhar no
+  download (rate limit). Liferay pode ter o ZIP mas nГЈo o catГЎlogo
+  atualizado. Um flat list[Source] nГЈo distingue esses papГ©is e
+  produzirГЎ gaps silenciosos de dados na janela de sobreposiГ§ГЈo.
+  
+  Adicionalmente (zai Round 2): INLABS conta dias CORRIDOS, nГЈo
+  Гәteis. Na virada de mГӘs com feriados, existe race condition onde
+  INLABS remove conteГәdo antes do ZIP mensal Liferay estar disponГӯvel.
+  
+  CorreГ§ГЈo obrigatГіria вҖ” SourceRouter com duas fases:
+  
+    @dataclass
+    class DiscoverySource:
+        name: str          # "INLABS" | "LIFERAY_CATALOG"
+        endpoint: str
+        probe: Callable    # HEAD/GET para verificar disponibilidade
+    
+    @dataclass  
+    class DownloadSource:
+        name: str          # "INLABS" | "LIFERAY_ZIP" | "CATALOG_JSON"
+        url: str
+        edition_meta: EditionMetadata  # propagado do discovery
+    
+    def resolve_discovery_sources(edition_date: date) -> list[DiscoverySource]:
+        """Onde buscar metadados/listagem."""
+        age = _calendar_days_age(edition_date)  # America/Sao_Paulo
+        if age <= 25:
+            return [INLABS_DISCOVERY]
+        elif age <= 40:  # overlap ampliado (Round 2: era 35, agora 40)
+            return [INLABS_DISCOVERY, LIFERAY_CATALOG]
+        else:
+            return [LIFERAY_CATALOG]
+    
+    def resolve_download_sources(
+        edition_date: date,
+        edition_meta: EditionMetadata
+    ) -> list[DownloadSource]:
+        """Onde baixar o payload, dado metadados jГЎ resolvidos."""
+        age = _calendar_days_age(edition_date)
+        sources = []
+        if age <= 40:
+            sources.append(INLABS_DOWNLOAD(edition_meta))
+        sources.append(LIFERAY_ZIP(edition_meta))
+        sources.append(CATALOG_JSON(edition_meta))
+        return sources
+  
+    - Downloader tenta fontes em ordem; primeira 200 vence
+    - Se TODAS as fontes falharem: estado EDITION_UNAVAILABLE com
+      retry em 24h (coluna unavailable_since no registry)
+    - Campo source registrado no registry: INLABS | LIFERAY_ZIP |
+      CATALOG_JSON
+    - Overlap ampliado para 40 dias (era 35): ZIP mensal Liferay pode
+      atrasar atГ© 10 dias Гәteis (~14 corridos); 40 dias cobre feriados
+    - Todas as comparaГ§Гөes de data: timezone America/Sao_Paulo explГӯcito
+    - Probe de disponibilidade: HEAD request com cache 1h por
+      (source, edition_date) para evitar chamadas repetidas (qwen)
+  
+    Circuit breaker (kimi suggestion, incorporado):
+    - ApГіs 3 erros 5xx consecutivos do INLABS em 5 minutos,
+      short-circuit INLABS por 30 minutos вҶ’ fallback direto Liferay
+    - Estado do circuit breaker em memГіria (single process, nГЈo precisa
+      persistir)
+  
+  LocalizaГ§ГЈo: src/backend/worker/source_router.py (NOVO),
+               src/backend/worker/discovery.py (REFACTOR),
+               src/backend/worker/downloader.py (REFACTOR)

+    - Todas as fontes 404 вҶ’ EDITION_UNAVAILABLE + retry 24h

+    - Timezone: consulta Г s 23:59 BRT vs 00:01 BRT do dia seguinte

+P1.5 в–ё RETRY: State-machine-aware com persistГӘncia atГҙmica
+  Consenso: 4/4 agentes, claude Round 2 refina atomicidade
+  Severidade consolidada: HIGH
+  
+  Problema (refinado Round 2):
+  (a) Retry metadata deve ser escrita atomicamente com state transition
+  (b) In-process sleep durante backoff perde retry_count se worker morre
+  (c) RETRY_PENDING deve ser restrito a estados com operaГ§Гөes transientes
+  
+  CorreГ§ГЈo:
+    TransiГ§ГЈo atГҙmica (single UPDATE, nГЈo multi-step):
+      UPDATE editions SET
+        state = 'RETRY_PENDING',
+        previous_state = :current_state,
+        retry_count = retry_count + 1,
+        next_attempt_at = :calculated_next,
+        last_error = :error_msg,
+        updated_at = datetime('now')
+      WHERE id = :id AND state = :current_state;  -- optimistic lock
+    
+    Retry-eligible states (restritos):
+      DOWNLOADING, EXTRACTING, INGESTING, VERIFYING
+      (NГғO: DISCOVERED, QUEUED, VERIFIED, CLEANED)
+    
+    Transient errors (retry):
+      HTTP 5xx, ConnectionError, Timeout, ES TransportError
+    Non-transient (fail imediato):
+      HTTP 404, HTTP 403, ValidationError, ParseError
+    
+    Backoff: delay = base * 2^attempt + random(0, base)
+    Config: MAX_RETRIES=3, BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS=5
+    
+    Worker loop (NOT in-process sleep):
+      1. Query: SELECT WHERE state='RETRY_PENDING'
+         AND next_attempt_at <= datetime('now')
+         ORDER BY next_attempt_at LIMIT 1
+      2. Re-entry: restore to previous_state, execute phase
+      3. Se max_retries excedido вҶ’ FAILED + watchdog notifica
+    
+    Index em (state, next_attempt_at) jГЎ definido em P1.0.

objections.location: Section P0.2 вҖ” SourceRouter resolve_sources()
objections.description: The SourceRouter conflates two distinct concerns: (1) where to discover edition metadata and (2) where to download the ZIP/XML payload. INLABS serves both an API for discovery (listing editions) and a download endpoint. Liferay serves a JSONWS catalog AND hosts ZIPs. The proposed resolve_sources() returns a flat list[Source] without distinguishing discovery vs download phases. This means the downloader will try to 'download from INLABS' even when only the catalog was needed from Liferay, or vice-versa. In the edge case where INLABS discovery succeeds but download fails (e.g., rate limit), the fallback list doesn't know to try Liferay download with the same edition metadata. This is a design error that will produce silent data gaps in the 25-35 day overlap window.
objections.suggested_fix: Split SourceRouter into two explicit phases: resolve_discovery_sources(edition_date) -> list[DiscoverySource] and resolve_download_sources(edition_date, edition_metadata) -> list[DownloadSource]. DiscoverySource provides catalog/listing endpoints. DownloadSource provides the actual ZIP/XML URL. The downloader receives the resolved metadata from discovery and independently resolves download sources. This also cleanly handles the case where INLABS discovery works but download is throttled вҖ” you fall back to Liferay download using the same edition identifiers.

objections.location: Section P1.1 вҖ” Deterministic ID scheme
objections.description: The proposed ID scheme sha256(f'{edition_date}|{section}|{page}|{ordinal}') is fragile. The DOU can republish or rectify atos, changing content on the same page/ordinal. Worse, 'ordinal' is an extraction artifact вҖ” if the XML parser changes order or the DOU restructures a section, every ID shifts. This makes the UNIQUE constraint a correctness hazard: legitimate updated atos get silently dropped by INSERT OR IGNORE. The artifact doesn't address republication/retificaГ§ГЈo at all, which is a known DOU pattern.
objections.suggested_fix: Use content-addressing for deduplication but identity-addressing for the primary key. Primary ID: sha256(f'{edition_date}|{section}|{act_type}|{act_number}') where act_number is the official ato identifier (e.g., 'PORTARIA NВә 123'). Dedup hash (separate column): sha256(normalized_body_text). On conflict of primary ID, UPDATE the record (upsert) rather than IGNORE вҖ” this handles retificaГ§Гөes. On conflict of dedup hash across different primary IDs, flag as potential duplicate for review. Add a 'version' counter column that increments on upsert.

objections.location: Section 4 вҖ” State Machine: RETRY_PENDING re-entry
objections.description: The state machine shows 'RETRY_PENDING вҶ’ estado anterior (re-entry)' but doesn't specify WHERE the state is stored atomically with the retry metadata. In SQLite with WAL, if the worker crashes between writing RETRY_PENDING and writing next_attempt_at, the entry is stuck in RETRY_PENDING forever with no scheduled re-attempt. The watchdog stale-check (>30min) won't catch this because RETRY_PENDING isn't listed as a stale-eligible state вҖ” only DOWNLOADING/EXTRACTING/INGESTING are. This is a liveness bug.
objections.suggested_fix: All state transitions including retry metadata (retry_count, next_attempt_at, last_error) must be written in a single UPDATE statement вҖ” no multi-step writes. Add RETRY_PENDING to the watchdog stale-check with a separate threshold: if next_attempt_at < now() - 5min AND state = RETRY_PENDING, treat as stuck and either re-trigger or escalate to FAILED. Document the exact SQL: UPDATE editions SET state='RETRY_PENDING', retry_count=retry_count+1, next_attempt_at=?, last_error=? WHERE id=? AND state=?

objections.location: Section P1.2 вҖ” ES mapping: dense_vector dims=1024 hardcoded
objections.description: Hardcoding dims=1024 with index=false is presented as 'zero cost' but it's not. The field exists in the mapping and will cause mapping conflicts when the actual model is chosen. If dims differ (768 for multilingual-e5-base, 384 for MiniLM), you cannot change dims on an existing mapping вҖ” you must reindex. Meanwhile, a dense_vector field with index=false still occupies mapping metadata and complicates index template management. The 'zero cost' claim is misleading.
objections.suggested_fix: Do NOT add the dense_vector field to v1 mapping at all. Instead, document the embedding strategy in an ADR (Architecture Decision Record) and plan for index aliasing: v1 index has no vector field, v2 (created when model is chosen) adds the correct dims. Use index aliases (dou-read, dou-write) from day one so the reindex + alias swap is seamless. This is genuinely zero cost and avoids a guaranteed future reindex caused by guessing dims wrong.

objections.location: Section P1.3 вҖ” Watchdog: Missing edition check 'dia Гәtil sem DOU atГ© 10h BRT'
objections.description: The watchdog's 'missing edition' check assumes DOU publishes every business day, but DOU has known exceptions: optional holidays (ponto facultativo), delayed publications, and extraordinary editions (ediГ§ГЈo extra) that publish on weekends. A naive 'is_business_day()' check will generate false-positive alerts on pontos facultativos and miss extraordinary editions entirely. The artifact doesn't specify the holiday calendar source or how to handle ediГ§Гөes extras.
objections.suggested_fix: Use a conservative approach: (1) maintain a minimal holiday list (feriados nacionais only вҖ” ~12 dates/year, easily hardcoded and updated annually), (2) for the missing-edition check, use a 2-hour grace period past the typical publication time (DOU usually publishes by 6h BRT, alert at 12h not 10h), (3) for extraordinary editions, the discovery phase should check INLABS with section filter for 'extra' editions on every run regardless of day-of-week, (4) alerts for missing editions should be WARNING not CRITICAL вҖ” a human should confirm before escalating.

objections.location: Section P0.1 вҖ” Downloader: disk space pre-check
objections.description: The 500MB minimum free disk check is a point-in-time check that's vulnerable to TOCTOU: another process (or concurrent extraction) can consume disk between the check and the download. On Fly.io with a shared volume, this is not theoretical вҖ” the extractor decompressing a previous edition's ZIP while the downloader starts a new one creates exactly this race.
objections.suggested_fix: Keep the pre-check but add a secondary guard: the streaming download should check available disk every N chunks (e.g., every 50MB written) and abort with a clean FAILED transition if free space drops below 200MB. Additionally, the pipeline should be strictly sequential per-edition (which the artifact implies but doesn't enforce): download вҶ’ extract вҶ’ ingest вҶ’ cleanup before starting the next edition. Enforce this with a single-flight lock in the scheduler.

objections.location: Section 5 вҖ” Sprint ordering: P1.5 before P1.1
objections.description: The sprint ordering puts SQLite PRAGMAs (P1.5) first, but the PRAGMA changes require testing under concurrency. Without the deterministic ID scheme (P1.1) and the retry columns (P1.4), there's nothing meaningful to test concurrent writes against. More critically, P1.2 (ES mapping) is in Sprint 2 but P0.2 (SourceRouter) is in Sprint 1 вҖ” the SourceRouter records 'source' in the registry, which depends on the schema having a 'source' column, which is defined in P1.2. Dependency chain is broken.
objections.suggested_fix: Reorder Sprint 1: (1) Schema migration first вҖ” add all new columns (deterministic_id, source, retry_count, last_error, next_attempt_at) + PRAGMAs in a single migration script, (2) P1.1 deterministic IDs, (3) P0.1 downloader streaming, (4) P0.2 SourceRouter, (5) P1.4 retry. Move the 'source' column addition out of P1.2 (ES mapping) into the schema migration. P1.2 should only cover ES-side concerns.

suggestions.description: The @retryable decorator approach is clean but consider making it state-machine-aware rather than a generic decorator. The retry should persist the attempt to SQLite BEFORE sleeping for backoff вҖ” if the worker is killed during backoff sleep, the retry_count is lost and resets to 0 on restart. Use a 'retry loop' pattern that reads next_attempt_at from the registry and skips entries whose next_attempt_at > now(), rather than an in-process sleep.

Rather than a badge saying 'Read-Only Dashboard', enforce it architecturally: the dashboard should query a read-only SQLite replica (using Litestream or Fly.io's built-in LiteFS) or at minimum a separate connection with ?mode=ro. The worker API should not even HAVE mutation endpoints that the dashboard could theoretically hit. If there's no mutation endpoint, there's no auth decision to make for the dashboard.

suggestions.location: Section 6 вҖ” Risk R3: ES single-node snapshots
suggestions.description: ES snapshots to S3/R2 require the repository-s3 plugin and credentials management. A simpler resilience strategy for a single-node setup: since SQLite is the source of truth and ES is a derived index, ensure the ingestor can fully rebuild the ES index from SQLite + stored XMLs (or re-downloaded ZIPs). Document the rebuild procedure and test it. This is cheaper and more reliable than managing ES snapshot infrastructure for a single-node deployment.

Promote this to P1 tier. Without structured logging with correlation IDs, debugging any of the P0/P1 issues in production on Fly.io will be done by grepping unstructured text in `fly logs`. Every P0/P1 implementation will benefit from having structured logging in place first. It's 2-3 hours of work and should be item 0 in Sprint 1.

objections.location: src/backend/worker/registry.py / fly.toml
objections.description: SQLite WAL configuration (P1.5) is ineffective and dangerous without explicit Fly.io Volume mount. Fly.io machines have ephemeral root filesystems; any restart, deployment, or machine replacement wipes the SQLite database located at /app or /tmp. This destroys the entire job registry and state machine, causing duplicate ingestion or total data loss.
objections.suggested_fix: Add P0 requirement: Mount Fly Volume at /data in fly.toml ([[mounts]] section). Force database path to /data/registry.db. Verify at startup that the path is writable and persists across SIGTERM/SIGINT.

objections.location: src/backend/worker/extractor.py
objections.description: Round 1 fixes ZIP streaming download but ignores XML extraction memory footprint. DOU XML files can exceed 500MB uncompressed. Using DOM-based parsing (ElementTree.parse or xml.dom.minidom) loads the entire tree into memory, triggering OOM-kill immediately after download and nullifying the streaming architecture.
objections.suggested_fix: Mandate iterative parsing using xml.etree.ElementTree.iterparse with strict element.clear() and parent.remove() after yielding each document node. Ensure extractor yields documents as a generator, maintaining constant memory regardless of XML file size.

objections.location: src/backend/api/search.py (missing architecture)
objections.description: Converged verdict incorrectly dismisses 'BM25-first hГӯbrido' as conflation with discovery. The user task explicitly requires this as a search-layer strategy for recent documents (вүӨ30 days). The verdict omits the query architecture entirely: no hybrid query template (combining BM25 text scores with reserved dense_vector knn), no recency boosting (decay function), and no routing logic between BM25-only (recent) and hybrid (historical) modes.
objections.suggested_fix: Add P1 requirement for Search API: Implement hybrid query using boolean should clauses or RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion). For recent documents (source=INLABS or ageвүӨ30), use BM25-only with script_score exponential decay on date field. For historical, combine BM25 + knn (weight 0 until embeddings ready). Expose via /search?strategy=recent|hybrid|auto.

objections.location: Section 6 (R1)
objections.description: Factual error in infrastructure sizing: Fly.io performance-1x provides 1GB RAM, not 2GB as stated. With Python overhead (3-5x for XML processing), streaming a 1GB ZIP requires ~2-3GB RAM. Recommending performance-1x guarantees OOM under production load.
objections.suggested_fix: Correct to performance-2x (2GB RAM) minimum for workers, or dedicated-cpu-1x with 2GB RAM. Validate with memory profiling using tracemalloc during 600MB fixture processing.

objections.location: src/backend/worker/es_mapping.py
objections.description: Proposes single static index 'v1' without ILM or rollover. DOU produces daily editions; at ~2k docs/day, this exceeds 700k docs/year. Single shard growth causes query latency degradation, long recovery times, and eventual shard size limits (50GB+). No index template or time-based routing defined.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement monthly indices (dou-2024-01, dou-2024-02) using date math in index names. Create index template for auto-provisioning. Use alias 'dou-write' for ingestion and 'dou-read' for dashboard searches spanning multiple indices.

suggestions.description: Add circuit breaker for INLABS: after 3 consecutive 5xx errors within 5 minutes, short-circuit INLABS attempts for 30 minutes to avoid hammering a degraded service, forcing immediate fallback to Liferay and reducing latency on degraded paths.

Add I/O pressure monitoring (iostat/iowait) to detect SQLite WAL contention on shared Fly.io volumes. Alert if iowait >40% for 2 minutes, as this indicates volume saturation masquerading as application health.

objections.location: src/backend/worker/source_router.py (proposto)
objections.description: SourceRouter usa idade fixa (25/35 dias) sem mecanismo de detecГ§ГЈo dinГўmica da disponibilidade real do INLABS. Se INLABS remover dados em 28 dias em vez de 30, o router continuarГЎ tentando INLABS inutilmente na zona de overlap.
objections.suggested_fix: Adicionar probe de disponibilidade por data no SourceRouter: tentar HEAD no INLABS para a ediГ§ГЈo especГӯfica antes de incluir na lista de fontes. Cache do resultado por 1h para evitar chamadas repetidas.

objections.location: src/backend/worker/downloader.py (proposto)
objections.description: Streaming para tempfile nГЈo especifica handling de download parcial/corrompido. Se conexГЈo cair em 90% do ZIP, o arquivo parcial permanece em disco sem detecГ§ГЈo atГ© extraГ§ГЈo falhar.
objections.suggested_fix: Implementar download com Content-Length validation prГ©via + verificaГ§ГЈo de bytes escritos vs esperado antes de marcar DOWNLOADED. Adicionar estado DOWNLOAD_CORRUPTED distinto de FAILED.

objections.location: src/backend/worker/registry.py (SQLite PRAGMAs)
objections.description: WAL mode em volumes Fly.io (network-attached storage) pode ter comportamento diferente de disco local. WAL files (-wal, -shm) podem nГЈo ser sincronizados corretamente em storage remoto.
objections.suggested_fix: Adicionar teste de concorrГӘncia real no deploy inicial: worker escrevendo + dashboard lendo simultaneamente por 10min. Fallback para DELETE mode se SQLITE_BUSY persistir.

objections.location: src/backend/worker/watchdog.py (proposto)
objections.description: Alertas Telegram para cada FAILED state causam alert fatigue. EdiГ§ГЈo com 500 atos pode gerar 500 alertas se todos falharem no ingest.
objections.suggested_fix: Implementar agregaГ§ГЈo de alertas: 1 alerta por ediГ§ГЈo com contagem de falhas, nГЈo por documento. Threshold configurГЎvel (ex: alerta sГі se >10% dos atos falharem).

objections.location: src/backend/worker/retry.py (proposto)
objections.description: Estado RETRY_PENDING requer query pattern nГЈo especificado: como o worker descobre quais entries estГЈo em RETRY_PENDING com next_attempt_at <= now? Polling eficiente nГЈo estГЎ definido.
objections.suggested_fix: Adicionar Гӯndice em (state, next_attempt_at) no SQLite. Worker faz query seletiva: SELECT WHERE state='RETRY_PENDING' AND next_attempt_at <= datetime('now'). Considerar fila separada para retries.

objections.location: src/backend/worker/ingestor.py (IDs determinГӯsticos)
objections.description: MigraГ§ГЈo de dados existentes nГЈo Г© coberta. Se jГЎ houver dados no ES/SQLite sem deterministic_id, o schema novo criarГЎ inconsistГӘncia entre histГіrico e novos dados.
objections.suggested_fix: Adicionar script de migraГ§ГЈo one-time: recalcula deterministic_id para entries existentes, detecta duplicatas potenciais, merge manual necessГЎrio antes de ativar UNIQUE constraint.

objections.location: spec original (BM25-first hГӯbrido)
objections.description: Veredito nГЈo define critГ©rio de transiГ§ГЈo de 'hГӯbrido BM25-first' para modo normal. Quando o histГіrico 'completa'? 30 dias? 90 dias? Quem decide?
objections.suggested_fix: Adicionar config TRANSITION_DAYS=90 no config.py. ApГіs 90 dias de operaГ§ГЈo, INLABS cobre todo o histГіrico relevante e fallback Liferay torna-se raro. Documentar decisГЈo.

suggestions.description: Adicionar mГ©trica de fonte usada (INLABS vs LIFERAY) no registry para monitorar eficГЎcia do router ao longo do tempo.
Incluir healthcheck endpoint que Fly.io possa usar para restart automГЎtico se worker travar (retorna 503 se stale > 1h).
Adicionar alias strategy (dou-articles-v1 вҶ’ dou-articles) para permitir reindex sem downtime futuro.
Especificar machine_type = 'performance-1x' (2GB RAM) como mГӯnimo, nГЈo 'cpu-1x-256mb'. Documentar custo mensal estimado.

objections.location: P0.2 SourceRouter - LГіgica de sobreposiГ§ГЈo dias 25-35
objections.description: A lГіgica de overlap ignora que INLABS conta dias CORRIDOS (30 dias calendГЎrio) enquanto publicaГ§ГЈo DOU Г© em dias ГҡTETEIS. Uma ediГ§ГЈo de sexta-feira consultada na segunda seguinte tem 'idade' de 3 dias calendГЎrio mas 1 dia Гәtil. Na virada de mГӘs com feriados, a janela de 25-35 dias pode ser insuficiente. Mais crГӯtico: nГЈo hГЎ tratamento para o race condition onde INLABS remove o conteГәdo EXATAMENTE aos 30 dias antes do ZIP mensal estar disponГӯvel no Liferay.
objections.suggested_fix: Adicionar lГіgica de fallback encadeado com tentativas: (1) INLABS вҶ’ se 404/timeout, (2) Liferay ZIP mensal вҶ’ se 404, (3) Catalog JSON individual вҶ’ se 404, marcar EDITION_UNAVAILABLE com retry em 24h. Adicionar coluna unavailable_since no registry para tracking. Considerar dias Гәteis no cГЎlculo da janela, nГЈo apenas dias corridos.

objections.location: P1.3 Watchdog - Telegram alerts sem rate limiting
objections.description: Telegram Bot API tem limite de ~30 mensagens/segundo e 20 mensagens/minuto para mesmo chat_id em grupos. Se o pipeline entrar em loop de falhas (ex: ES down), o watchdog pode disparar dezenas de alertas em minutos, causando rate limit do Telegram e silenciamento completo do canal de alertas justamente quando mais necessГЎrio.
objections.suggested_fix: Implementar alert deduplication + rate limiting: (1) Hash do alerta (tipo + contexto) como chave, (2) Cooldown de 5min entre alertas idГӘnticos, (3) Burst limit de 10 alertas/minuto, (4) Overflow вҶ’ digest aggregado a cada 15min, (5) Fallback para log crГӯtico se Telegram retornar 429. Adicionar mГ©trica 'alerts_suppressed' para visibility.

objections.location: State Machine proposta - TransiГ§Гөes para RETRY_PENDING nГЈo especificadas
objections.description: O diagrama mostra 'Qualquer estado вҶ’ RETRY_PENDING' mas isso Г© semanticamente incorreto. Estados como DISCOVERED, QUEUED, VERIFIED, CLEANED nГЈo tГӘm operaГ§Гөes de rede que justificam retry. Permitir retry nesses estados mascararia bugs de lГіgica. AlГ©m disso, nГЈo hГЎ especificaГ§ГЈo de qual estado Г© o 'estado anterior' para re-entry apГіs RETRY_PENDING.
objections.suggested_fix: Restringir RETRY_PENDING aos estados com operaГ§Гөes transientes: DOWNLOADING, EXTRACTING (se IO/parse), INGESTING, VERIFYING. Adicionar coluna previous_state no SQLite para restaurar estado correto apГіs retry. Documentar transiГ§Гөes vГЎlidas em tabela explГӯcita.

objections.location: State Machine - Estado CLEANED sem tratamento de falha
objections.description: O estado CLEANED assume sucesso da operaГ§ГЈo de cleanup. Se o cleanup falhar (ex: arquivo em uso, permissГЈo, disco read-only), nГЈo existe estado CLEAN_FAILED. O item permaneceria em VERIFIED enquanto temp files persistem, causando acГәmulo de disco silencioso.
objections.suggested_fix: Adicionar estado CLEANING (transitГіrio) e CLEAN_FAILED. Em CLEAN_FAILED: log + watchdog alert + retry de cleanup no prГіximo ciclo. Alternativamente: cleanup Г© best-effort com watchdog alert se disco > threshold, sem estado dedicado.

objections.location: P0.1 Downloader - SHA-256 sem fonte de comparaГ§ГЈo
objections.description: O veredito propГөe 'hashlib incremental (SHA-256) para integridade' mas nem INLABS nem Liferay fornecem checksums para comparaГ§ГЈo. Calcular SHA-256 sem comparar com valor conhecido Г© security theater вҖ” detecta apenas corrupГ§ГЈo de transferГӘncia, nГЈo adulteraГ§ГЈo. Se o objetivo Г© detectar corrupГ§ГЈo, o ZIP tem CRC interno que Г© mais apropriado.
objections.suggested_fix: OpГ§ГЈo A: Validar ZIP integrity via zipfile.testzip() apГіs download вҖ” detecta corrupГ§ГЈo usando CRC32 interno do ZIP. OpГ§ГЈo B: Se SHA-256 for mandatГіrio para audit trail, documentar que Г© para detecГ§ГЈo de mudanГ§a entre downloads do mesmo arquivo (re-download), nГЈo para validaГ§ГЈo contra fonte externa. Renomear campo de 'integrity_hash' para 'content_fingerprint'.

objections.location: SeГ§ГЈo 5 Ordem de ExecuГ§ГЈo - ConcorrГӘncia de workers nГЈo endereГ§ada
objections.description: SQLite WAL permite mГәltiplos readers mas APENAS UM writer simultГўneo. O documento nГЈo especifica se o design assume (a) worker single-thread, (b) mГәltiplos workers com serializaГ§ГЈo de writes, ou (c) mГәltiplas machines Fly.io. Se (b) ou (c), hГЎ race condition potencial no discovery + state transitions sem advisory locks.
objections.suggested_fix: Documentar explicitamente: 'Design assume single worker process'. Se mГәltiplos workers forem necessГЎrios, adicionar: (1) SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE para writes, (2) advisory lock file, ou (3) Redis lock distribuГӯdo. Adicionar verificaГ§ГЈo de lock no startup do worker com erro fatal se jГЎ em execuГ§ГЈo.

objections.location: P1.1 IDs DeterminГӯsticos - ColisГЈo potencial em republicaГ§Гөes
objections.description: O esquema sha256(f"{edition_date}|{section}|{page}|{ordinal}") nГЈo considera que DOU pode republicar o mesmo ato com correГ§Гөes em ediГ§Гөes subsequentes (retificaГ§Гөes). Um ato retificado teria ID diferente por data diferente, mas se a correГ§ГЈo for publicada na mesma data (improvГЎvel mas possГӯvel em ediГ§Гөes extras), haveria colisГЈo ou sobrescrita.
objections.suggested_fix: Adicionar campo 'retification_flag' ao schema de ID se DOU suporta mГәltiplas versГөes do mesmo ato na mesma data. Alternativamente, documentar que retificaГ§Гөes sГЈo tratadas como documentos distintos (comportamento atual Г© aceitГЎvel). Validar com anГЎlise de 6 meses de DOU para identificar frequГӘncia de retificaГ§Гөes.

suggestions.description: Adicionar teste especГӯfico para o cenГЎrio 'consulta Г s 23:59 do dia 30' vs 'consulta Г s 00:01 do dia 31' вҖ” timezone matters. Especificar que todas as comparaГ§Гөes de data devem usar timezone BRT (America/Sao_Paulo) explГӯcito, nГЈo UTC.

O healthcheck GET /health deve retornar payload JSON com campos: status (ok/degraded/down), last_cycle_at, pending_count, failed_count, disk_free_mb, es_status. Fly.io machine checks que recebem 503 por 3 minutos consecutivos podem triggerar restart automГЎtico.

Adicionar que o recommendation de performance-1x (2GB RAM) deve ser validado com teste de pico: 3 ediГ§Гөes DOU simultГўneas (DOU1, DOU2, DOU3) com ZIPs totais > 1GB. Se ES tambГ©m roda na mesma machine, RAM compartilhada pode ser gargalo.
Mover P3.6 para P1.6. Constantes mГЎgicas espalhadas pelo cГіdigo dificultam ajuste fino em produГ§ГЈo. Config centralizada Г© prereq para operaГ§ГЈo autГҙnoma, nГЈo melhoria de backlog.

P0.2 в–ё DISCOVERY/DOWNLOAD: Roteamento temporal com separaГ§ГЈo de fases
  Consenso: 4/4 agentes, claude Round 2 adiciona critical refinement
  Severidade consolidada: CRITICAL
  
  Problema (refinado Round 2): O SourceRouter Round 1 conflata duas
  preocupaГ§Гөes distintas:
    (1) DISCOVERY: onde obter metadados/listagem de ediГ§Гөes
    (2) DOWNLOAD: onde obter o ZIP/XML payload
  
  INLABS pode responder discovery (listar ediГ§Гөes) mas falhar no
  download (rate limit). Liferay pode ter o ZIP mas nГЈo o catГЎlogo
  atualizado. Um flat list[Source] nГЈo distingue esses papГ©is e
  produzirГЎ gaps silenciosos de dados na janela de sobreposiГ§ГЈo.
  
  Adicionalmente (zai Round 2): INLABS conta dias CORRIDOS, nГЈo
  Гәteis. Na virada de mГӘs com feriados, existe race condition onde
  INLABS remove conteГәdo antes do ZIP mensal Liferay estar disponГӯvel.
  
  CorreГ§ГЈo obrigatГіria вҖ” SourceRouter com duas fases:
  
    @dataclass
    class DiscoverySource:
        name: str          # "INLABS" | "LIFERAY_CATALOG"
        endpoint: str
        probe: Callable    # HEAD/GET para verificar disponibilidade
    
    @dataclass  
    class DownloadSource:
        name: str          # "INLABS" | "LIFERAY_ZIP" | "CATALOG_JSON"
        url: str
        edition_meta: EditionMetadata  # propagado do discovery
    
    def resolve_discovery_sources(edition_date: date) -> list[DiscoverySource]:
        """Onde buscar metadados/listagem."""
        age = _calendar_days_age(edition_date)  # America/Sao_Paulo
        if age <= 25:
            return [INLABS_DISCOVERY]
        elif age <= 40:  # overlap ampliado (Round 2: era 35, agora 40)
            return [INLABS_DISCOVERY, LIFERAY_CATALOG]
        else:
            return [LIFERAY_CATALOG]
    
    def resolve_download_sources(
        edition_date: date,
        edition_meta: EditionMetadata
    ) -> list[DownloadSource]:
        """Onde baixar o payload, dado metadados jГЎ resolvidos."""
        age = _calendar_days_age(edition_date)
        sources = []
        if age <= 40:
            sources.append(INLABS_DOWNLOAD(edition_meta))
        sources.append(LIFERAY_ZIP(edition_meta))
        sources.append(CATALOG_JSON(edition_meta))
        return sources
  
    - Downloader tenta fontes em ordem; primeira 200 vence
    - Se TODAS as fontes falharem: estado EDITION_UNAVAILABLE com
      retry em 24h (coluna unavailable_since no registry)
    - Campo source registrado no registry: INLABS | LIFERAY_ZIP |
      CATALOG_JSON
    - Overlap ampliado para 40 dias (era 35): ZIP mensal Liferay pode
      atrasar atГ© 10 dias Гәteis (~14 corridos); 40 dias cobre feriados
    - Todas as comparaГ§Гөes de data: timezone America/Sao_Paulo explГӯcito
    - Probe de disponibilidade: HEAD request com cache 1h por
      (source, edition_date) para evitar chamadas repetidas (qwen)
  
    Circuit breaker (kimi suggestion, incorporado):
    - ApГіs 3 erros 5xx consecutivos do INLABS em 5 minutos,
      short-circuit INLABS por 30 minutos вҶ’ fallback direto Liferay
    - Estado do circuit breaker em memГіria (single process, nГЈo precisa
      persistir)
  
  LocalizaГ§ГЈo: src/backend/worker/source_router.py (NOVO),
               src/backend/worker/discovery.py (REFACTOR),
               src/backend/worker/downloader.py (REFACTOR)
  EsforГ§o: ~8h
  Testes obrigatГіrios:
    - Dia 25, 29, 30, 31, 35, 40, 60
    - Virada de mГӘs: ediГ§ГЈo 2024-01-31 consultada em 2024-02-01

P1.1 в–ё IDs DETERMINГҚSTICOS: Esquema robusto com suporte a retificaГ§Гөes
  Consenso: 4/4 agentes, claude Round 2 refina esquema
  Severidade consolidada: HIGH
  
  Problema (refinado): O esquema Round 1 usa page+ordinal вҖ” ambos sГЈo
  artefatos de parsing instГЎveis. Se o XML parser muda ordenaГ§ГЈo ou
  DOU reestrutura seГ§ГЈo, IDs mudam. INSERT OR IGNORE descarta
  retificaГ§Гөes legГӯtimas silenciosamente.
  
  CorreГ§ГЈo:
    Esquema de ID em duas camadas:
    
    1. Identity key (primary, determinГӯstico):
       deterministic_id = sha256(f"{edition_date}|{section}|{act_type}|{act_identifier}")
       Onde act_identifier Г© o identificador oficial do ato extraГӯdo
       do XML (ex: "PORTARIA NВә 123", "INSTRUГҮГғO NORMATIVA NВә 45").
       Truncado a 32 hex вҶ’ prefixo "dou-{hex32}"
    
    2. Content fingerprint (dedup):
       content_fingerprint = sha256(normalized_body_text)
       Coluna separada para detecГ§ГЈo de mudanГ§a de conteГәdo.
    
    Comportamento no conflito:
    - Mesmo deterministic_id + mesmo content_fingerprint вҶ’ SKIP (idempotente)
    - Mesmo deterministic_id + content_fingerprint diferente вҶ’ UPSERT
      (retificaГ§ГЈo: incrementa version, atualiza body no ES)
    - deterministic_id diferente + mesmo content_fingerprint вҶ’ FLAG
      como potencial duplicata (log warning, ingerir normalmente)
    
    SQL:
      INSERT INTO editions (...) VALUES (...)
      ON CONFLICT(deterministic_id) DO UPDATE SET
        content_fingerprint = excluded.content_fingerprint,
        version = version + 1,
        state = 'DISCOVERED',
        updated_at = datetime('now')
      WHERE excluded.content_fingerprint != editions.content_fingerprint;
    
    Fallback para atos sem identificador oficial (avisos genГ©ricos):
      act_identifier = f"page_{page}_ord_{ordinal}" 
      (documentar como caso degradado; ~5% dos atos)
  
  LocalizaГ§ГЈo: src/backend/worker/id_generator.py (NOVO),
               src/backend/worker/registry.py,
               src/backend/worker/ingestor.py
  EsforГ§o: ~4h
  Teste: discovery 2x mesma data вҶ’ 0 linhas novas; retificaГ§ГЈo вҶ’

P1.5 в–ё RETRY: State-machine-aware com persistГӘncia atГҙmica
  Consenso: 4/4 agentes, claude Round 2 refina atomicidade
  Severidade consolidada: HIGH
  
  Problema (refinado Round 2):
  (a) Retry metadata deve ser escrita atomicamente com state transition
  (b) In-process sleep durante backoff perde retry_count se worker morre
  (c) RETRY_PENDING deve ser restrito a estados com operaГ§Гөes transientes
  
  CorreГ§ГЈo:
    TransiГ§ГЈo atГҙmica (single UPDATE, nГЈo multi-step):
      UPDATE editions SET
        state = 'RETRY_PENDING',
        previous_state = :current_state,
        retry_count = retry_count + 1,
        next_attempt_at = :calculated_next,
        last_error = :error_msg,
        updated_at = datetime('now')
      WHERE id = :id AND state = :current_state;  -- optimistic lock
    
    Retry-eligible states (restritos):
      DOWNLOADING, EXTRACTING, INGESTING, VERIFYING
      (NГғO: DISCOVERED, QUEUED, VERIFIED, CLEANED)
    
    Transient errors (retry):
      HTTP 5xx, ConnectionError, Timeout, ES TransportError
    Non-transient (fail imediato):
      HTTP 404, HTTP 403, ValidationError, ParseError
    
    Backoff: delay = base * 2^attempt + random(0, base)
    Config: MAX_RETRIES=3, BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS=5
    
    Worker loop (NOT in-process sleep):
      1. Query: SELECT WHERE state='RETRY_PENDING'
         AND next_attempt_at <= datetime('now')
         ORDER BY next_attempt_at LIMIT 1
      2. Re-entry: restore to previous_state, execute phase
      3. Se max_retries excedido вҶ’ FAILED + watchdog notifica
    
    Index em (state, next_attempt_at) jГЎ definido em P1.0.
  
  LocalizaГ§ГЈo: src/backend/worker/retry.py (NOVO),
               src/backend/worker/registry.py
  EsforГ§o: ~4h
  Teste: HTTP 503 durante download вҶ’ RETRY_PENDING вҶ’ re-entry em
         DOWNLOADING вҶ’ sucesso; worker kill durante backoff вҶ’ restart вҶ’
         retry executa no tempo correto
